_comment: Interpreter-tier eshost-cli host registry for Cynic's cross-engine
micro-bench harness. Use with `eshost -c bench/eshost-hosts.json ...`
or copy the `hosts` block into ~/.eshost-config.json.
This harness is an INTERNAL regression compass, not a public
scoreboard. It compares Cynic's bytecode interpreter against other
engines' INTERPRETER TIERS ONLY. Every JIT engine below is pinned
to its no-JIT flags so the comparison stays apples-to-apples:
Cynic has no JIT, so a JIT-warm peer would be a meaningless baseline.
Engine paths assume jsvu's default layout (~/.jsvu/bin/<engine>).
Install with: npm i -g jsvu eshost-cli && jsvu --engines=quickjs,v8,spidermonkey,hermes
No-JIT flag rationale:
v8 (d8)        --jitless          disable all JIT tiers, interpreter-only
spidermonkey   --no-baseline --no-ion   disable Baseline + Ion JITs
(older builds also accept --no-warp; jsvu 151.x rejects it)
quickjs-ng     none               natively a non-JIT C interpreter
hermes         none               natively interpreter-only
cynic          none               pure bytecode interpreter, no JIT
QuickJS-NG is the headline peer: a non-JIT C interpreter, the
fairest comparison point. Cynic already vendors its libregexp.
Host-type notes: jsvu's QuickJS-NG 0.14.0 rejects the `-N` flag the
eshost `qjs` agent injects, so QuickJS is registered with the `ch`
host type (bare file arg, auto-detects scripts, exposes print()).
The cross-engine timing runner (tools/bench-cross.sh) invokes the
engine binaries directly with these same flags rather than through
eshost's agent layer, because the jsvu binary versions and the
bundled eshost agents drift apart (hermes -fenable-tdz, etc.).
This config still drives manual `eshost --list` / one-off eshost
runs and documents the canonical flag set in one committed place.
